items.title: Former University of Michigan researcher accused of hiding Chinese military drone ties
items.snippet: Investigators say Chuan Wang claimed modest roles on visa applications when in fact he was publicly known in China for his military drones.

items.title: US Army to receive thousands of Barracuda-500M cruise missiles in Anduril deal
items.snippet: The Pentagon’s Low-Cost Containerized Missiles program aims to obtain over 10,000 low-cost cruise missiles over a three-year span.

items.title: No sound of silence: US soldiers train eyes — and ears — for drone swarms
items.snippet: The U.S. Army is moving beyond battling individual drone threats as it experiments with tactics to combat throngs of unmanned aircraft in saturated skies.

items.title: USS Gerald R. Ford to return from 11-month deployment on Saturday
items.snippet: The world’s largest aircraft carrier is finally returning home after etching its name into Navy history books.

items.title: US Marines practice seizing remote islands in Philippine exercise
items.snippet: Based on Oahu, MADIS crews face limitations when live-firing at home, so Konien was enthusiastic about the opportunity to fire rounds and missiles abroad.

items.title: Near Russian border, NATO grapples with ground robots in combat

items.title: Blacklists, corruption and frontline needs: Ukraine tackles an arms-export puzzle
items.snippet: After years of struggling to arm its one million active-duty soldiers, Kyiv has been wary of allowing its domestic producers to sell their weapons abroad.

items.title: Israel to extend F-35I range amid war with Iran

items.title: Japan fires first-ever missiles from Philippine soil
items.snippet: The first Type 88 missile hit its target – a former minesweeper dating from WWII – 47 miles away after a six-minute flight.

items.title: US Navy could run out of money by July, top officer warns
items.snippet: Chief of Naval Operations Adm. Daryl Caudle told lawmakers at a budget hearing Tuesday that the Navy might have to modify training and operations.

items.title: Contractor awarded $3.5 billion to build out US Coast Guard’s Arctic Security Cutters fleet
items.snippet: The five cutters to be delivered by Davie Defense will represent a new class of Arctic icebreakers.

items.title: Air Force MQ-9 fleet drops to 135 aircraft after Iran combat losses

items.title: Pentagon reaches agreements with defense firms on containerized missiles

items.title: US special operations leaders frustrated by inability to modify their own equipment
items.snippet: The military must wait for manufacturers to fix or upgrade equipment, leaving them with outdated capabilities as technology evolves quickly, they said.
